summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is simple harmonic motion (SHM)?

Displacement is the straight-line distance from start to finish with direction (vector). It differs from distance, which is the total path length (scalar). The answer is 'Periodic motion with restoring force proportional to displacement: F = −kx'. The other options — 'Any periodic motion', 'Motion in a circle', and 'Motion at constant speed' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is the period of a mass-spring system?

Hooke's Law: F = -kx, where k is the spring constant and x is displacement from equilibrium. Elastic PE = ½kx². The answer is 'T = 2π√(m/k)'. The other options — 'T = 2π√(k/m)', 'T = mk', and 'T = 2πmk' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is the period of a simple pendulum?

The period of a simple pendulum T = 2π√(L/g) depends only on length (L) and gravitational acceleration (g). It does NOT depend on mass or amplitude (for small angles). The answer is 'T = 2π√(L/g)'. The other options — 'T = 2π√(g/L)', 'T = 2πLg', and 'T = L/g' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is the maximum speed in SHM?

Maximum speed = Aω (at equilibrium). The other options — 'v_max = A/ω', 'v_max = Aω²', and 'v_max = ω/A' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.
